---
title: "multiple static ips can't access with external network"
date: 2009-10-18
forum: Server Platforms
---

### Post by citium on 2009-10-18
hi,

I have around 8 server.
And each of them when I restart,in ifconfig it says it up.

But when I ping it from my computer it not accessible. 

i have to use ifup, ifdown and ifconfig repeatedly.

any suggestion would be appreciated.

here are one of my configuration.

/etc/udev/rules.d/70-persistent-net.rules 
```

# This file was automatically generated by the /lib/udev/write_net_rules
# program run by the persistent-net-generator.rules rules file.
#
# You can modify it, as long as you keep each rule on a single line.

# PCI device 0x14e4:0x164c (bnx2)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="eth*", NAME="local"

# PCI device 0x14e4:0x164c (bnx2)
SUBSYSTEM=="net", ACTION=="add", DRIVERS=="?*", ATTR{address}=="XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX", ATTR{type}=="1", KERNEL=="eth*", NAME="public"

```

/etc/network/interface
```

auto lo
iface lo inet loopback

# Local private network
auto local
iface local inet static
	address 192.168.0.101
	netmask 255.255.255.0
	network 192.168.0.1
	broadcast 192.168.0.255
	gateway 192.168.0.2



# The primary network interface
auto public
iface public inet static
	address XX.XX.XX.114
	netmask 255.255.255.224
	network XX.XX.XX.96
	broadcast XX.XX.XX.127
	gateway XX.XX.XX.97
	# dns-* options are implemented by the resolvconf package, if installed
	dns-nameservers 4.2.2.1
	dns-search XXX


# Free download IP
auto public:cow
iface public:cow inet static
	address XX.XX.XX.112
	netmask 255.255.255.224
	network XX.XX.XX.96
	broadcast XX.XX.XX.127
	gateway XX.XX.XX.97
	# dns-* options are implemented by the resolvconf package, if installed
	dns-nameservers 4.2.2.1
	dns-search XXX


# IP for private use
auto public:dog
iface public:dog inet static
	address XX.XX.XX.113
	netmask 255.255.255.224
	network XX.XX.XX.96
	broadcast XX.XX.XX.127
	gateway XX.XX.XX.97
	# dns-* options are implemented by the resolvconf package, if installed
	dns-nameservers 4.2.2.1
	dns-search XXX

```

---

### Post by citium on 2009-10-21
Problem solve (Support From Lease web, excellent customer service keep me inform every 30 min).

removing extra information from the alias will solve the issue.

	network XX.XX.XX.96
	broadcast XX.XX.XX.127
	gateway XX.XX.XX.97
	# dns-* options are implemented by the resolvconf package, if installed
	dns-nameservers 4.2.2.1
	dns-search XXX

> 

auto lo
iface lo inet loopback

# Local private network
auto local
iface local inet static
	address 192.168.0.101
	netmask 255.255.255.0
	network 192.168.0.1
	broadcast 192.168.0.255
	gateway 192.168.0.2



# The primary network interface
auto public
iface public inet static
	address XX.XX.XX.114
	netmask 255.255.255.224
	network XX.XX.XX.96
	broadcast XX.XX.XX.127
	gateway XX.XX.XX.97
	# dns-* options are implemented by the resolvconf package, if installed
	dns-nameservers 4.2.2.1
	dns-search XXX


# Free download IP
auto public:cow
iface public:cow inet static
	address XX.XX.XX.112
	netmask 255.255.255.224


# IP for private use
auto public:dog
iface public:dog inet static
	address XX.XX.XX.113
	netmask 255.255.255.224


---
